question_text: In the context of sound waves, what is meant by the term 'amplitude'?
correct_answer: Amplitude is the maximum displacement of particles from their resting position as the sound wave passes through a medium. It is related to the loudness or intensity of the sound.
explanation: Amplitude measures how far particles move from their starting position when a sound wave passes through them. Imagine plucking a guitar string - if you pluck it hard (large amplitude), it vibrates a long way from the middle and makes a loud sound. If you pluck it gently (small amplitude), it barely moves and makes a quiet sound.
